sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal.

Piel-

(1) to pervert, Exo 23:8, וִיסַלֵּף דִּבְרֵי צַדִּיקִים “and (a gift) perverts the words of the righteous,” Deu 16:19. Pro 19:3, אִוֶּלֶת אָדָם תְּסַלֵף דַּרְכֹּו “the foolishness of a man perverteth his way.”

(2) to overturn, to send headlong, Job 12:19 Pro 21:12 13:6 22:12 .

[“The primary force of this root has of late been correctly laid down by Fasius, following Alb. Schultens and Arnold (Neue Jahrb. i. p. 168), to be that of slipperiness, and gliding away, escaping.-

(1) trans. to slip away (fchlüpfen, to slip).-(2) intrans. to be slippery (fchlüpfrig fehn), comp. Arab. سلف to pass by (vorbehfchlüpfen) and to daub over, both taken from that of slipperiness, and with the sibilant turned into an aspirate, חָלַף to pass by, to leave behind (prop. vorbehfchlüpfen), Gr. ἀλείφω. Hence-

Piel-

(1) to cause to slip, or fail, as a just cause (σφάλλειν δίκαν, Eurip. Androm. 781 al. 766), Exo 23:8 Pro 13:6, רִשְׁעָה תְּסַלֵּף חַטָּאת “wickedness causes the erring (foot) to slip.” Hence to give to destruction, Deu 16:19 Pro 21:12 22:12 Job 12:19.-(2) to make (a way) slippery, Pro 15:3.” Ges. App.] Hence-

[“סֶלֶף m. prop. smoothness, slipperiness; hence flattery, nearly i.q. חֶלְקָה Pro 11:3 15:4 .” Ges. App.]
